entries.evidence: User request to bootstrap the quant-validation-agent harness with directory structure, policies, tools, middleware, tests, and docs.
entries.root_cause: No quantitative validation harness existed in the repository.
entries.targeted_fix: Create CLAUDE.md, README, harness policy files (system_prompt, data_contract, metric_policy, validation_workflow, threshold_policy, permission_policy, change_manifest schema/file), docs, skills, subagents, tools, middleware, tests, examples, memory and report folders.
entries.expected_benefit: Standardized, reproducible, observable quantitative validation for scoring / PD / LGD / EAD / scenario regression models.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive scaffolding under a new top-level directory; no operational system is touched.
entries.validation_method: pytest run on tools and middleware unit tests; manual review of policy and docs files.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the quant-validation-agent directory if scaffolding is rejected; downgrade unsuccessful changes by file-level reverts and append a rollback entry.

entries.evidence: Improvement #1 from completion summary — monitoring scenarios required a coded time × grade matrix.
entries.root_cause: skills/monitoring_validation.md described the procedure but no concrete tool was implemented.
entries.targeted_fix: Add functions build_time_grade_matrix, compute_period_psi_vs_baseline, summarize_default_rate_trend that work on row-level or pre-aggregated data.
entries.expected_benefit: Automates per-period × grade summaries needed for ongoing monitoring; reduces ad-hoc pandas code per validation.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive module; no existing function altered.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_monitoring_table.py covers row-level and aggregated paths plus baseline error handling.

entries.evidence: Improvement #2 — time-series regression of PD multipliers/scenario models needs autocorrelation and heteroskedasticity tests.
entries.root_cause: Initial diagnostics only covered VIF/condition index/JB; time-series concerns (autocorrelation, ARCH) were absent.
entries.targeted_fix: Add calculate_durbin_watson, calculate_breusch_godfrey, calculate_arch_test using statsmodels; manual fallback for DW.
entries.expected_benefit: Better detection of misspecification in scenario regressions where residuals are typically autocorrelated.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — new functions only; existing diagnostics untouched.
entries.validation_method: pytest cases against IID and AR(1) synthetic samples confirm DW < 1.5 and BG p-value < 0.05 on autocorrelated residuals.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the three new functions from regression_diagnostics.py and the corresponding tests.

entries.evidence: Improvement #3 — PD bias and similar metrics where both over- and under-prediction are bad require an absolute-value RAG mode.
entries.root_cause: assign_rag_status only supported higher_is_better and lower_is_better.
entries.targeted_fix: Add 'abs_lower_is_better' direction; reject negative thresholds for that direction; add tests covering both signs.
entries.expected_benefit: Correct RAG assignment for two-sided metrics like PD bias.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — existing two directions remain unchanged; new branch is opt-in.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_validation_summary.py covers all three directions and threshold validation errors.
entries.rollback_rule: Revert assign_rag_status to two-direction form and delete new tests.

entries.evidence: Improvement #4 — skills/challenger_model_validation.md described the comparison but had no automation.
entries.root_cause: Champion vs challenger comparisons were manual.
entries.targeted_fix: Add check_same_sample, compare_discrimination, compare_calibration with explicit same-sample assumption checks before metric deltas.
entries.expected_benefit: Reduces error-prone manual comparison code; enforces same-sample assumption.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_challenger_compare.py covers identical/different samples, discrimination delta, and calibration deltas.

entries.evidence: Improvement #5 — reproducibility benefits from a discoverable command-line entrypoint.
entries.root_cause: Validation runs were invoked ad-hoc via Python imports without a single entrypoint.
entries.targeted_fix: Create quant_validation_agent package with __main__/cli exposing 'run', 'thresholds', 'check' subcommands; subprocess-based pytest verifies behavior.
entries.expected_benefit: Consistent invocation surface; permission/PII guards usable as a CI gate via 'check'.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — CLI is new and does not modify tools/middleware logic.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_cli.py runs the CLI as a subprocess for run/thresholds/check.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the quant_validation_agent directory and tests/test_cli.py.

entries.evidence: Improvement #6 — markdown-only thresholds were not machine-readable and not auditable as code.
entries.root_cause: threshold_policy.md held reference numbers but they could not be looked up programmatically.
entries.targeted_fix: Externalize thresholds into harness/threshold_policy.json; add a read-only loader; document the JSON in threshold_policy.md; reference it from CLI.
entries.expected_benefit: Threshold changes become a single-file edit captured in change_manifest with human approval; loader prevents typos.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — values mirror the existing markdown reference; loader is read-only and never mutates the file.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_threshold_loader.py validates load, lookup, model-type listing, and missing-file error.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the JSON, loader, and tests; restore threshold_policy.md original wording.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #1 — calibration was descriptive only; needed inferential tests.
entries.root_cause: metric_calibration.py produced calibration tables and Brier but no formal goodness-of-fit tests.
entries.targeted_fix: Add Hosmer-Lemeshow chi-square test, Spiegelhalter Z test, and per-bucket binomial calibration test with explicit sample-size guards.
entries.expected_benefit: Stronger evidence base for PD calibration assessments; reduces over-reliance on bin-by-bin eyeballing.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive module, scipy already in dependencies.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests against well-calibrated and miscalibrated synthetic samples.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete tools/calibration_test.py and the test file.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #2 — KS plot coordinates and lift table were missing as ready-to-use functions.
entries.root_cause: build_decile_table existed but did not produce cumulative gain/lift or KS plot coordinates.
entries.targeted_fix: Add build_lift_table (cumulative gain, lift, base-rate normalized) and ks_plot_coordinates (rank_pct, cum_bad, cum_good, ks_distance).
entries.expected_benefit: Faster reporting of lift and KS visualization data; KS plot max distance equals calculate_ks output (verified).
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies first-bucket lift > 1, total cumulative shares = 1, and KS plot max == calculate_ks.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete tools/decile_lift.py and the test file.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #4 — different segments (retail, SME, LDP corporate) need different thresholds.
entries.root_cause: Original JSON had a single global metric block; segment-specific thresholds had to be reapplied manually.
entries.targeted_fix: Add by_segment.<seg>.metrics.<metric> overlay; loader.get_metric_threshold(segment=...) returns overlaid values, falls back to global, and reports the source.
entries.expected_benefit: Auditable, declarative segment-level RAG without code changes.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive structure; existing global-only callers behave identically because segment defaults to None.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies override values, fallback to global, and unknown-segment fallback.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove by_segment block from JSON and revert loader to the global-only signature.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #3 and #6 — single-command end-to-end execution and a recurring-finding writer were missing.
entries.root_cause: Validation runs and recurring-issue notes required custom Python scripts.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `validate` subcommand (scoring/PD: KS/AUROC/AR/PSI; LGD/EAD: MAE/RMSE/bias) with RAG via threshold_loader and segment overrides. Add `note add` subcommand that appends to memory/recurring_validation_findings.md and refuses guard violations (returns 5).
entries.expected_benefit: One-shot reproducible execution; recurring findings accumulate in a structured, guard-protected file.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Medium — new commands change CLI surface, but existing commands (run/thresholds/check) are unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest subprocess tests cover validate (scoring + lgd), missing-columns failure, segment override, note happy path, and note PII rejection.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove cmd_validate, cmd_note, and their parser entries; remove related tests.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #5 — manifest entries should be schema-validated automatically.
entries.root_cause: Manifest entries were validated manually; future edits could violate the schema unnoticed.
entries.targeted_fix: Add jsonschema-based test that validates every entry, asserts unique change_ids, and constrains status values. Add jsonschema to project dependencies.
entries.expected_benefit: Continuous protection against malformed or duplicated manifest entries.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — adds a dependency, but jsonschema is widely available.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_change_manifest.py runs in the standard suite.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the test file and the jsonschema dependency.

entries.evidence: Improvement-related housekeeping — segment overlay should be documented near the markdown reference table.
entries.root_cause: threshold_policy.md mentioned the JSON file but not the by_segment overlay.
entries.targeted_fix: Append a short note pointing readers to the by_segment block in threshold_policy.json.
entries.expected_benefit: Easier discovery of segment-specific thresholds for human reviewers.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #1 — scenario regression validation required wiring fitting + diagnostics + severity + floors into one orchestrator.
entries.root_cause: Existing tools handled each step but practitioners had to repeatedly assemble the pipeline.
entries.targeted_fix: Add fit_scenario_regression / predict_scenarios / check_scenario_severity / check_multiplier_floors / run_pipeline; expose `validate-scenario` in the CLI; add a small synthetic historical CSV.
entries.expected_benefit: One-shot scenario regression validation with all standard diagnostics; lower error rate vs hand-built pipelines.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Medium — orchestrator is new code, but underlying tools were already tested.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests for fit / predict / severity / floor / end-to-end pipeline; CLI subprocess test runs the example data with supplied multipliers.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the new module, tests, sample_macro_history.csv, and the validate-scenario subparser; remove cmd_validate_scenario from cli.py.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #2 — LGD RMSE/bias and EAD-ratio thresholds were missing, leaving CLI metrics Gray.
entries.root_cause: Initial JSON only defined mae_lgd; EAD has no portable absolute threshold across portfolios.
entries.targeted_fix: Add rmse_lgd, bias_lgd, mae_ead_ratio, rmse_ead_ratio, bias_ead_ratio thresholds. Update CLI validate to compute LGD MAE/RMSE/bias with thresholds and EAD errors normalized by mean realized (ratio metrics).
entries.expected_benefit: CLI now produces RAG for LGD and EAD, eliminating 'policy_undefined' Gray for these models.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — JSON additions and CLI branch changes; existing scoring/PD path unaffected.
entries.validation_method: Updated and new pytest cases assert RAG is in {Green, Yellow, Red} for LGD; EAD reports include mae_ratio/rmse_ratio/bias_ratio with RAG.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the new threshold entries and revert the EAD branch in cmd_validate to raw-Gray.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #3 — Hosmer-Lemeshow on sparse / LDP samples produced empty bins.
entries.root_cause: Quantile binning could yield buckets smaller than the χ² cell-size rule of thumb.
entries.targeted_fix: Add min_per_bin parameter performing greedy sort-and-pack so each bin has >= min_per_bin observations; tail merged into previous bin if under-filled. Reject min_per_bin < 1 and samples too small for two bins.
entries.expected_benefit: Stable HL test on small / sparse / LDP samples; explicit binning='greedy_min_per_bin' tag in output.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — quantile-binning behavior unchanged when min_per_bin is omitted.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers sparse-sample packing, too-small input rejection, and invalid min_per_bin.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #4 — CLI runs were not auto-recorded by run_logger.
entries.root_cause: Logging was available as a Python helper but not exposed at the CLI surface.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --log-dir to validate and validate-scenario; on success, write a structured run log via middleware.run_logger.write_run_log.
entries.expected_benefit: Each CLI execution produces a timestamped JSON record under logs/ for audit.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — only triggered when --log-dir is provided.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies a run_*.json file is written and contains request_summary / main_results.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove --log-dir parser entries and the run_logger calls.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #5 — lift tables needed a markdown formatter for reports.
entries.root_cause: Reports were produced manually from the DataFrame.
entries.targeted_fix: Add format_lift_markdown(lift_df, decimals=4); empty-DF placeholder; column-validation error.
entries.expected_benefit: Direct copy-paste of lift tables into validation reports.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers happy path, empty DF placeholder, missing-columns error.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the new function and tests.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #6 — operations team requested a future-ready monotonic-timestamp invariant for manifest entries.
entries.root_cause: Strict ordering had not yet been agreed upon.
entries.targeted_fix: Add an opt-in pytest test gated by environment variable QVA_STRICT_MANIFEST that asserts non-decreasing timestamps in append order. Default test run is unaffected.
entries.expected_benefit: When operations enables the env var, mis-ordered manifest entries fail CI immediately.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — test is skipped by default.
entries.validation_method: Manually exercised with QVA_STRICT_MANIFEST=1; default suite runs unchanged.
